title: Named Account Support Playbook
description: Design named-account support: agents permanently or primarily tied to account sets (service account manager style), with a model for commercial pods and large single-segment pools.

mindset.principle: Random queue is fair to the ACD and cruel to the relationship. Named ownership is how commercial accounts stop re-explaining every week.

mistakes.label: Every call any agent for strategic accounts
mistakes.cost: No memory; sales loses trust; repeat diagnosis

mistakes.label: One person owns everything; no bench
mistakes.cost: Bus factor; burnout; vacation collapses service

mistakes.label: Pods never share patterns
mistakes.cost: Same bug fixed five ways; no product signal

models.label: Small set of agents wholly tied to a set of commercial accounts
One large segment (e.g. institutional) as one named pool / group
Named primary with overflow rules to trained backup

decision_branches.situation: One huge segment behaves like one account
Only one person knows the account

steps.name: List accounts that need named ownership
Map agents to sets; publish to sales

agent_signal.use_when: Need service-account or named-agent support for commercial or strategic accounts. Random queue failing relationship. Large segment as one dedicated pool.
decision_criteria: Map agents to account sets, not only skills.
Always bench and notes; never one hero.
Share patterns across pods so structure does not silo learning.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: SAM-style commercial pods; large gov/institutional as one big named group.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic job spine only. Not HR legal advice on titles/pay.
trigger_phrases: Need service-account or named-agent support for commercial or strategic accounts
Need playbook: Named Account Support Playbook
